author: bharath.vedartham
content:
yeah i did consider that, the reject cases are:
```
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.builder_index` is a valid/active builder index -- i.e.
  `is_active_builder(state, bid.builder_index)` returns `True`. (1)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.execution_payment == 0`. (2)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.fee_recipient == proposer_preferences.fee_recipient`. (3)
- _[REJECT]_ The length of KZG commitments is less than or equal to the
  limitation defined in the consensus layer -- i.e. validate that
  `len(bid.blob_kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(compute_epoch_at_slot(bid.slot)).max_blobs_per_block`. (4)
- _[REJECT]_ The bid is for a higher slot than its parent block -- i.e. validate
  that `bid.slot` is greater than the slot of the block with root
  `bid.parent_block_root`. (5)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.prev_randao` is the correct RANDAO mix -- i.e. validate that (6)
  `bid.prev_randao == get_randao_mix(parent_state, get_current_epoch(parent_state))`.
- _[REJECT]_ `signed_execution_payload_bid.signature` is valid with respect to
  the `bid.builder_index`. (7)
```
I dont think (1), (2), (4), (5), (7)  are possible.  I wonder if we are setting the fee recipient incorrectl

author: bharath.vedartham
content: but i assume the entire devnet has 1 fee recipient so its hard to get that wrong lmao

author: bharath.vedartham
content: unclear if the randao i m sending is wrong too. i m not sure if buildoor is doing anything wrong here. let me investigate more

author: nflaig
content: we aren't validating the randao yet on p2p though, that's a new gossip rule

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i m not sure what the warn means here

author: nflaig
content: yeah that's a funny thing I noticed before, so we publish the block first, then peers use getblobs and get them from EL then disseminate columns before we actually publish the payload, so in some cases we receive them via gossip before, so during publish it's tracked as duplicate and the publish count stays 0 hence the warning
